denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 04/18/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W103523955          Group #: GRP-1445
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-164986
Claim #: CLM-2026-211586
Date of Service (requested): 03/29/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Northline Medical Group

Dear Plan Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 03/31/2026 for Spironolactone for androgenic symptoms related to Polycystic ovary syndrome with hyperandrogenism (E28.2). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Spironolactone for androgenic symptoms
Diagnosis (as submitted): Polycystic ovary syndrome with hyperandrogenism (E28.2)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM105 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM105 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Clinical criteria for Spironolactone for androgenic symptoms are not met per the cited utilization guideline module for Polycystic ovary syndrome with hyperandrogenism (E28.2). The request does not satisfy threshold requirements in the referenced criteria set.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with guideline mis cite. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a UnitedHealthcare medical director before initiating a formal appeal on the same authorization decision.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W103523955) and reference UM-2026-164986. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 49-year-old member has Polycystic ovary syndrome with hyperandrogenism (E28.2). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Spironolactone for androgenic symptoms recommended as the next appropriate step. Failed OCP trial due to migraine aura; hirsutism score elevated. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that guideline mis cite bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings (including dated scale scores in the chart) that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic UHC correspondence with UnitedHealthcare medical policy and procedural tone.
